diseases.severity_impact_on_medicine_use: HIGH — affects renal dosing (metformin, sitagliptin), glucose-altering drug interactions, contraindications for corticosteroids
HIGH — interacts with NSAIDs (reduce effect), affects ARB/ACE inhibitor safety, requires caution with decongestants
CRITICAL — metformin, NSAIDs, aminoglycosides, contrast agents contraindicated or dose-adjusted; many drugs require eGFR-based dosing
CRITICAL — statins, metformin, clopidogrel, telmisartan contraindicated; affects drug metabolism via CYP enzymes
HIGH — aspirin/NSAIDs can trigger bronchospasm in aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease; beta-blockers contraindicated
HIGH — QT-prolonging drugs (azithromycin, ciprofloxacin) require caution; NSAIDs increase cardiovascular risk
HIGH — fluoroquinolones, tramadol lower seizure threshold; many antiepileptics interact with other drugs via CYP induction
MEDIUM — levothyroxine absorption affected by calcium, iron, PPIs; amiodarone and lithium affect thyroid function
HIGH — NSAIDs, aspirin, corticosteroids, bisphosphonates are contraindicated or need gastroprotection
CRITICAL — many drugs contraindicated; teratogenicity risk; see pregnancy_rules.json for per-drug classifications
HIGH — all penicillin-class antibiotics contraindicated; cross-reactivity with cephalosporins possible
HIGH — beta-blockers contraindicated in severe COPD; opioids/sedatives may worsen respiratory depression
